card.intuition: Executing CPU-intensive JavaScript code in parallel to the main thread, preventing the Event Loop from becoming blocked.
card.analogy: A busy restaurant kitchen: the head chef (Main Thread) handles the orders and customer communication (Event Loop), but they hire 'Prep Cooks' (Worker Threads) to chop 50kg of onions in a separate room so the kitchen doesn't slow down.
when_to_use: Image processing or video encoding in Node.js
Parsing giant JSON or CSV files that would freeze the UI/API
gotchas: Passing giant amounts of data to a worker can be slow because it's usually CLONED, not SHARED (unless using SharedArrayBuffer)
Each Worker starts its own V8 instance, which has significant memory overhead (startup cost)

exercise.prompt: Complete the Node.js pseudocode for sending data to and from a worker thread.
exercise.test_code:
def check(pm, m): return pm == "postMessage" and m == "message"
assert check("postMessage", "message")
print("PASS")
